subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Shared Responsibility Model
description: A fast breakdown of The Shared Responsibility Model for builders.

hook.text: When your cloud app gets hacked, whose fault is it? The answer depends on which service model you use.

buildup.text: Cloud providers and customers split responsibility. The provider secures the infrastructure below you.

discovery.text: With IaaS, you secure the OS, apps, and data. With SaaS, the provider handles almost everything.

twist.text: Most cloud breaches happen because customers misconfigure their part — not because the provider failed.

climax.text: Understanding where your responsibility starts is the first step to real cloud security.

punchline.text: The cloud is secure. Your configuration might not be.

quiz.question: What causes most cloud security breaches?
options: Customer misconfiguration, not cloud provider failures
Hackers breaking through cloud provider firewalls
